content: We can reframe open-world graph anomaly detection as an intrinsically motivated exploration task, where an ARC-based generalist graph anomaly detector leverages an ICM curiosity mechanism to assign intrinsic reward to out-of-distribution subgraph structures that deviate from learned cross-entity correlations, proactively discovering rare novel anomalies instead of only scoring pre-defined candidate nodes. This enables the one-for-all generalist detector to continuously improve its anomaly detection capability on unseen graph domains without additional labeled anomaly data.
